description: House rules and deviations from D&D 5e RAW used in this simulation.

critical_hits.rule: Maximum damage on first die, roll second
critical_hits.description: On a critical hit, the first damage die is maximized and only the additional die is rolled, instead of rolling all dice twice.
critical_hits.replaces: PHB p.196 — roll all dice twice on a crit

death_saving_throws.rule: Allies can stabilize with a Medicine check (DC 12)
death_saving_throws.description: An adjacent ally can use their action to make a DC 12 Wisdom (Medicine) check to stabilize a downed creature, instead of requiring a healer's kit.
death_saving_throws.replaces: PHB p.197 — healer's kit required to automatically stabilize

flanking.rule: Flanking grants +2 to attack rolls (not advantage)
flanking.description: When two allies have a creature directly between them, each gains +2 to melee attack rolls against that creature instead of advantage, to reduce the dominance of advantage stacking.
flanking.replaces: DMG optional flanking rule — grants advantage

short_rest.description: Short rests take 10 minutes instead of 1 hour to make them more practical in dungeon crawl scenarios.
short_rest.replaces: PHB p.186 — short rest is 1 hour

potions.rule: Potions can be drunk as a bonus action
potions.description: Drinking a potion yourself costs a bonus action. Administering a potion to another creature still costs an action.
potions.replaces: PHB — drinking a potion costs an action

ability_checks.rule: Ties on contested checks go to the initiating character
ability_checks.description: When a contested ability check results in a tie, the character who initiated the action succeeds.
ability_checks.replaces: PHB p.174 — ties go to the defender

inspiration.rule: Inspiration can be spent after seeing the roll result
inspiration.description: Players may choose to spend Inspiration to reroll after seeing the result of a d20, but before the outcome is narrated.
inspiration.replaces: PHB — Inspiration declared before the roll
